justifications.ip1: All three ACs have distinct, specific failing test scenarios: T1/T2 cover AC1 (RBNZ match → blocked, AUSTRAC not called), T3 covers AC2 (RBNZ clear + AUSTRAC match → AUSTRAC_WATCHLIST), and T4/T5 cover AC3 (both clear → not blocked with audit log).
justifications.ip2: The plan creates only `src/aml/dual-aml-screener.js` and `tests/aml/dual-aml-screener.test.js` as specified; the Notes section explicitly lists 'Payment routing, SWIFT notification, AUSTRAC transaction reporting, FX reporting, DIA registration — all excluded,' with no out-of-scope tasks present.
justifications.ip3: Every test (T1–T8) names the specific file (`tests/aml/dual-aml-screener.test.js`), concrete mock return values, and exact expected return shapes; the implementation file (`src/aml/dual-aml-screener.js`) specifies precise function logic with named imports and explicit sequential `await` calls.
justifications.ip4: The plan presents the full test suite in a RED Phase section before the GREEN Phase implementation, preserving test-first order throughout, but does not include an explicit 'run tests to confirm RED' execution step between the test-writing and implementation phases.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 (C7 sequential ordering) is enforced by T6 with a call-sequence tracking assertion (`expect(callSequence).toEqual(['rbnz', 'austrac'])`), NFR-2 (C8 audit on every path) is enforced by T5/T7/T8, and the implementation explicitly comments 'NO Promise.all()' as a compiled-in constraint with no configurable parameter introduced.

notes: Exceptionally thorough plan with concrete test assertions covering all ACs and both NFRs, explicit C7 sequential-ordering enforcement via call-sequence tracking, and complete C8 audit-path coverage across all three outcome branches; only minor gap is the absence of an explicit RED-phase test-run step.
